text:
Imagine a moonlit forest. A deer stands still, its antlers glowing softly. It inhales deeply—a sigh of tranquility amidst nocturnal whispers—but then the wind picks up. A sharp crackling sound interrupts the night. The deer leaps into the air, flees—swift as thought—from the forest edge into the deep shadow of the undergrowth.

A small figure races after, their eyes widening at the eerie silence. It's a hobbit clutching a flashlight. They leap over fallen branches with nimble feet. With each heartbeat it seems louder—a drumroll for an adventure.

After a minute they emerge into a clearing where a glimmering pool reflects stars. The hobbit peeks into the water.

There—a creature. Half-animal, half-god. Its body stretches elongated limbs into silken feathers. It floats softly atop the water.

The hobbit's mouth opens wide. They speak in wonder:

"Elonged birds?!"

The creature arches its neck
